channel.title: NYT > Arts > Television
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Taylor Frankie Paul’s Past Was No Secret. A Child’s Cry Changed Everything.
item.description: Before being cast as “The Bachelorette,” Taylor Frankie Paul had discussed — with police, on podcasts and on TV — the domestic dispute that involved her 5-year-old daughter.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs and John Koblin
item.category: The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives (TV Program)

item.title: ‘For All Mankind’ Is the Anti-‘Black Mirror’
item.description: The optimistic space-race drama, starting its fifth season, imagines humans settling space without tech colonizing our minds.
item.dc:creator: James Poniewozik

item.title: How ‘Love Story’ Gave Viewers a History Lesson on the Kennedys and ’90s NYC
item.description: The FX show, which dramatizes the relationship between John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette, has inspired a legion of younger viewers to dig into the couple’s past and 1990s New York City.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez
item.category: Love Story: John F Kennedy Jr & Carolyn Bessette (TV Program)

item.title: Don’t Let the Name Harry Hole Fool You. The Important One Is Jo Nesbo.
item.description: After a disappointing movie adaptation, the Norwegian author took the reins as showrunner in a new Netflix series based on his Detective Hole books.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli
item.media:credit: David B. Torch for The New York Times
item.media:description: The actor Tobias Santelmann, left, stars at the title character in the writer Jo Nesbo’s “Detective Hole,” a new series-length Netflix adaptation, which Nesbo oversaw.

item.title: Marriage Might Not Happen. But ‘Something Very Bad’ Did.
item.description: Executive produced by Matt and Ross Duffer, a new series by Haley Z. Boston finds horror in an idea that already terrifies her generation: romantic commitment.
item.dc:creator: Erik Piepenburg and Sela Shiloni
item.category: Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Sela Shiloni for The New York Times
item.media:description: “Where is my fear of commitment coming from?” Boston recalled asking herself as she developed the story. “I was like, Let me go super deep into it and, you know, broadcast it to the world.”

item.title: ‘Bait’ Review: Bond. Shahjehan Bond.
item.description: Riz Ahmed imagines the dark side of being a 007 candidate (a position he knows something about) in his Prime Video comedy series.
item.dc:creator: Mike Hale

item.title: The Kennedy Center Is Giving Bill Maher the Mark Twain Prize
item.description: The annual award that recognizes merit in humor is going to the satirist and talk show host who has been critical of President Trump and political correctness.
item.dc:creator: Ben Sisario and Marc Tracy
item.category: Mark Twain Prize for American Humor
Real Time With Bill Maher (TV Program)
Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Hunter Abrams for The New York Times
item.media:description: “Thank you to the Mark Twain people: I just had the award explained to me, and apparently it’s like an Emmy, except I win,” Bill Maher said in a statement.

item.title: The ‘Daredevil: Born Again’ Showrunner Feels Your Pain, Really
item.description: That big death at the start of Season 1 was hard for Dario Scardapane, too. Season 2 might not be any easier.
item.dc:creator: Connie Chang
item.media:credit: Tommy Rizzoli for The New York Times
item.media:description: “It’s been a lot more satisfying to start from whole cloth in Season 2,” said Dario Scardapane, who had to take over “Daredevil: Born Again” midway through production of Season 1.

item.title: How ‘One Piece’ Fixed a Common Problem for Anime Adaptations
item.description: The Netflix hit uses a combination of craft and collaboration to tackle one of the trickiest challenges for live-action anime adaptations: wigs.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Motamayor

item.title: ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island,’ Plus 7 Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: A new iteration of the Bravo franchise begins and the second season of “Your Friends & Neighbors” premieres.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez
item.media:description: A still from “The Real Housewives of Rhode Island.”

item.title: ‘The Comeback’ Season 3, Episode 2: Getting the Band Back Together
item.description: Valerie doesn’t fully understand why people in Hollywood are so worked up about A.I. She just sees a wave she thinks she knows how to ride.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray
item.media:description: The director James Burrows, as himself, with Lisa Kudrow, as Valerie Cherish, in a scene from “The Comeback.”

item.title: Eric Overmyer, Who Wrote for Modern Television Classics, Dies at 74
item.description: Trained as a playwright, he got his first TV writing job on “St. Elsewhere,” then worked on “Homicide: Life on the Street,” “The Wire,” “Treme” and “Bosch.”
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir
item.category: Homicide: Life on the Street (TV Program)
item.media:description: Eric Overmyer in 1994. “His scripts have a remarkable ability to thrill, enlighten and bemuse,” the writer and television producer Tom Fontana said.

item.title: James Tolkan, a Tough-Talking Actor in ‘Back to the Future’ and ‘Top Gun,’ Dies at 94
item.description: Mr. Tolkan’s career spanned decades but his breakout roles came as an authority figure in two popular films of the mid-1980s.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Ortiz
item.media:description: As Cmdr. Tom “Stinger” Jardian in “Top Gun,” Mr. Tolkan gives a verbal lashing to young pilots who lacked discipline.

item.title: Have We Been Mispronouncing Thoreau’s Name for Centuries?
item.description: George Clooney, Meryl Streep and other voice actors had to be persuaded, but a new PBS documentary (mostly) leads by example in stressing the first syllable.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lyall

item.title: Jon Bernthal and Ebon Moss-Bachrach on Bringing ‘Dog Day Afternoon’ to Broadway
item.description: Jon Bernthal and Ebon Moss-Bachrach are both making their Broadway debut in a high-stakes adaptation of the beloved 1975 film “Dog Day Afternoon.”
item.dc:creator: Melena Ryzik
item.media:credit: Jonah Rosenberg for The New York Times

item.title: Stream These Movies and Shows Before They Leave Netflix in April
item.description: Several Christopher Guest mockumentaries and almost every James Bond movie are among the titles leaving for U.S. subscribers before the month ends.
item.dc:creator: Jason Bailey

item.title: Want More ‘Love Story’? Read These Books Inspired by the Kennedys and ’90s New York.
item.description: If the TV show has you craving 1990s glam, upper-crust romance and doomed dynasties, these books have got you covered.
item.dc:creator: Sadie Stein
item.category: Love Story: John F Kennedy Jr & Carolyn Bessette (TV Program)
Once Upon a Time: The Captivating Life of Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy (Book)

item.title: Late Night Disses Trump’s New ‘America First’ Award
item.description: “How many fake trophies that were made specifically for him is this guy going to get?” Seth Meyers asked on Thursday’s “Late Night.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: ‘The Pitt’ Season 2, Episode 12 Recap: Breaking Points
item.description: The stalwarts of the Pitt seem to be cracking under the stress of an especially difficult day — in a workplace defined by difficult days.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins
item.media:description: Dana (Katherine LaNasa) is losing her composure. She isn’t the only one.

item.title: Carole Radziwill on FX’s ‘Love Story’ and Returning to ‘Real Housewives’
item.description: Her deceased loved ones are characters on a hit TV show, her name is in the Epstein files and she’s returning to “Real Housewives.” What does she make of it all?
item.dc:creator: Iva Dixit
item.category: The Real Housewives of New York (TV Program)
Love Story: John F Kennedy Jr & Carolyn Bessette (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Lucia Bell-Epstein for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rebutting others’ accounts of her deceased loved ones — her husband, Anthony Radziwill; his cousin, John F. Kennedy, Jr.; and Carolyn Bessette Kennedy — has long been something of a duty for Carole Radziwill. But even as she has readily taken up that cause, she is also tired of doing it.

item.title: Josh Johnson Ridicules the Army’s Opening to Older Recruits
item.description: “Look, according to People magazine — and apparently the U.S. military — 42 is the new 35,” Johnson said on Tuesday’s “Daily Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Late Night Questions the ‘Very Big Present’ Trump Got From Iran
item.description: Josh Johnson was puzzled by what kind of gift the president could have received “from the people you are currently at war with.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Mel Schilling, Dating Expert on ‘Married at First Sight,’ Dies at 54
item.description: Ms. Schilling, an Australian psychologist, was diagnosed with colon cancer in 2023. Two weeks ago she said the disease had spread to her brain.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses
item.media:description: Mel Schilling at the 2025 BAFTA Awards.

item.title: Comedy’s Most Manipulative Shot … and Its Greatest Weapon
item.description: Audience reactions are a staple of standup specials. But they’re a strange device when you take a closer look.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman

item.title: Late Night Doesn’t Feel So Hot About ICE at the Airport
item.description: Jimmy Kimmel said President Trump had “found a way to make the airport even worse.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
Real Time With Bill Maher (TV Program)

item.title: John Lithgow as Roald Dahl in ‘Giant’: A Study in Monstrosity
item.description: In Mark Rosenblatt’s play, a powerful portrayal of the beloved children’s book author who almost gleefully exposes his bigotry.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw
item.media:description: John Lithgow in “Giant” at the Music Box Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: Valerie Perrine, Screen Siren Who Won Critical Acclaim, Dies at 82
item.description: Known early on for skin-baring temptress roles, she later earned rave reviews, a Cannes award and an Oscar nomination for her performance in the Lenny Bruce biopic “Lenny.”
item.dc:creator: Alex Williams
item.media:credit: Chic Donchin/American Broadcasting Companies, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Valerie Perrine in “Malibu,” a 1983 television movie on ABC. “Unlike most sex symbols of the past,” one critic wrote, she “has almost always been singled out by the critics for her acting rather than her anatomy.”

item.title: Bill Cosby Loses Sex Assault Lawsuit and Faces a $59 Million Judgment
item.description: A California jury found that Mr. Cosby had abused Donna Motsinger in 1972 after inviting her to attend one of his comedy shows.
item.dc:creator: Graham Bowley and Rachel Parsons
item.media:description: Bill Cosby was accused in the civil suit of sexually assaulting Donna Motsinger in 1972 when he was a celebrated entertainer and she was a waitress in a restaurant he visited.

item.title: Lilias Folan, Who Brought Yoga to Middle America, Dies at 90
item.description: With her bright leotards and soothing, welcoming tone, she helped to demystify a discipline that many Americans in the 1970s viewed as a counterculture practice.
item.dc:creator: Jeré Longman
item.media:credit: Duane Howell/The Denver Post, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Ms. Folan in 1976. “I’ve always known I was put on this planet to do this sharing and this journey,” she said.

item.title: A Review of the U.K.’s ‘S.N.L.’ Debut From Both Sides of the Atlantic
item.description: In a country with little knowledge of the American TV institution, the show landed in a new light. An American and a British critic found plenty to enjoy.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman and Gabriel Tate

item.title: ‘Real Housewives’ Has Been a Meme Gold Mine for 20 Years
item.description: The Bravo TV empire, which turns 20 this month, has also been a gold mine for the internet.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor
item.category: The Real Housewives of Orange County (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Elizabeth Renstrom for The New York Times
